extraction.one_line_summary: JACOBSEN Fabio won stage 4 sprint after ALPECIN Fenix forced early acceleration uphill, allowing DECEUNINCK Quick-Step to wait and counter.
tactical_patterns: ALPECIN Fenix initiated sprint early on 6% finishing ramp forcing rivals to commit before JACOBSEN could time perfect counter.
DEMAR Arnaud sprinted best on wide-open boulevards without positioning battles, signals route profile matters more than raw power.
PARAME Rein crashed after 3km barrier when JUMBO rider crossed wheels, positioning shield required even for GC leaders.
Intermediate sprint avoided by PHILIPSEN may have preserved energy for stage finish compared to competitors who contested it.

rider_signals.JACOBSEN Fabio: positive: demonstrated elite sprint timing and mental resilience after near-fatal Poland crash, waited longest before launching
rider_signals.DEMAR Arnaud: positive: strong sprint on wide finishing straight suggests tactical advantage when course avoids tight technical finishes
rider_signals.PHILIPSEN Jasper: neutral: skipped intermediate sprint points unlike JACOBSEN and MATTHEWS, energy conservation strategy unclear if beneficial
rider_signals.PARAME Rein: negative: crashed through no fault after 3km mark when shielded inadequately, team protection deficit exposed
